2605.00505.title: LLM-Oriented Information Retrieval: A Denoising-First Perspective
2605.00505.paper_summary: Modern information retrieval (IR) is no longer consumed primarily by humans but increasingly by large language models (LLMs) via retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and agentic search. Unlike human users, LLMs are constrained by limited attention budgets and are uniquely vulnerable to noise; misleading or irrelevant information is no longer just a nuisance, but a direct cause of hallucinations and reasoning failures. In this perspective paper, we argue that denoising-maximizing usable evidence density and verifiability within a context window-is becoming the primary bottleneck across the full information access pipeline. We conceptualize this paradigm shift through a four-stage framework of IR challenges: from inaccessible to undiscoverable, to misaligned, and finally to unverifiable. Furthermore, we provide a pipeline-organized taxonomy of signal-to-noise optimization techniques, spanning indexing, retrieval, context engineering, verification, and agentic workflow. We also present research works on information denoising in domains that rely heavily on retrieval such as lifelong assistant, coding agent, deep research, and multimodal understanding.
2605.00505.paper_summary_zh: 現代的信息檢索（IR）不再主要由人類使用，而是越來越多地由大型語言模型（LLMs）通過檢索增強生成（RAG）和代理搜索來使用。與人類用戶不同，LLMs受到有限注意力預算的限制，並且對噪音特別敏感；誤導性或不相關的信息不再僅僅是一種煩擾，而是幻覺和推理失敗的直接原因。在這篇觀點文章中，我們主張，去噪-最大化可用證據密度和可驗證性在上下文窗口內，正在成為整個信息訪問管道的主要瓶頸。我們通過一個四階段的IR挑戰框架來概念化這一範式轉變：從不可訪問到不可發現，再到不對齊，最後到不可驗證。此外，我們提供了一個管道組織的信號對噪音優化技術分類法，涵蓋了索引、檢索、上下文工程、驗證和代理工作流程。我們還展示了在依賴檢索的領域（如終身助手、編碼代理、深度研究和多模態理解）中有關信息去噪的研究工作。
2605.00505.author: Lu Dai et.al.
2605.00505.authors: Lu Dai, Liang Sun, Fanpu Cao, Ziyang Rao, Cehao Yang, Hao Liu, Hui Xiong
